hook.text: Transaction A writes a value but hasn't committed. Transaction B reads it. If A rolls back, B used phantom data.

buildup.text: Read Uncommitted allows dirty reads: seeing data from uncommitted transactions.

discovery.text: It's the lowest isolation level. Maximum concurrency, minimum safety.

twist.text: Postgres doesn't actually implement Read Uncommitted. It silently upgrades to Read Committed.

climax.text: Almost no production application uses Read Uncommitted. The risk of dirty reads is too high.

punchline.text: Read Uncommitted: fast but dangerous. Rarely used in practice.

quiz.question: What is a dirty read?
options: Reading data from a transaction that hasn't been committed yet and might roll back
Reading data from a corrupted table
Reading data that has been deleted
